info.name: agentic-ReAct API
info.description:
ReAct Agent API — FastAPI + SSE Streaming

## Auth
Every request requires the header `X-API-Key: dev-key-12345` (default dev key).

The `X-API-Key` is NOT an OpenAI/Anthropic key — it is the application-level API key defined in `VALID_API_KEYS` inside your `.env` file.

## SSE Stream (POST /api/v1/chat)
The response is a Server-Sent Events stream. Each line starting with `data:` contains a JSON payload.

Event types:
- `thought`      — agent's reasoning step
- `action`       — tool being called (name + input)
- `observation`  — tool result
- `final_answer` — conclusive answer
- `done`         — stream ended (contains conversation_id)
- `error`        — structured error (code + detail + retry flag)

## Seed data (sql/init.sql)
### Shipments
| ID      | Tracking          | Status            | Route                    | Carrier      | User     |
|---------|-------------------|-------------------|--------------------------|--------------|----------|
| shp-001 | TRK-2024-000001   | in_transit        | Madrid, ES → Paris, FR   | DHL Express  | user-001 |
| shp-002 | TRK-2024-000002   | delivered         | Barcelona → London       | FedEx        | user-001 |
| shp-003 | TRK-2024-000003   | pending           | Berlin → Rome            | UPS Freight  | user-002 |
| shp-004 | TRK-2024-000004   | out_for_delivery  | Amsterdam → Warsaw       | GLS          | user-002 |
| shp-005 | TRK-2024-000005   | processing        | Lisbon → Brussels        | TNT          | user-003 |
| shp-006 | TRK-2024-000006   | in_transit        | Vienna → Prague          | DHL Freight  | user-003 |
| shp-007 | TRK-2024-000007   | returned          | Stockholm → Helsinki     | PostNord     | user-004 |
| shp-008 | TRK-2024-000008   | cancelled         | Dublin → Edinburgh       | An Post      | user-005 |
| shp-009 | TRK-2024-000009   | delivered         | Zurich → Munich          | Swiss Post   | user-005 |
| shp-010 | TRK-2024-000010   | in_transit        | Copenhagen → Oslo        | DB Schenker  | user-001 |

### Users
| ID       | Username        | Role                | Department  |
|----------|-----------------|---------------------|-------------|
| user-001 | alice.johnson   | logistics_manager   | Logistics   |
| user-002 | bob.martinez    | operator            | Operations  |
| user-003 | carol.white     | compliance_officer  | Compliance  |
| user-004 | david.kim       | admin               | IT          |
| user-005 | eva.rodriguez   | operator            | Logistics   |

variable.description: Application-level API key (NOT an OpenAI/Anthropic key). Defined in VALID_API_KEYS in .env
Set this after a /chat call using the conversation_id from the 'done' SSE event

request.description:
Returns service status. No authentication required.

Expected response:
```json
{"status": "ok", "service": "agentic-ReAct"}
```

item.name: 1 · Agent — Core (Parte 2.1)
item.description: Tests for the ReAct agent with Function Calling and tool chaining (Parte 2.1 of the technical evaluation).

request.description:
## Simple ERP query — Parte 2.1

Demonstrates the agent invoking the `get_erp_data` tool with `action=get_shipment`.

### Expected SSE flow
```
event: thought
data: {"content": "I need to query the ERP for shipment shp-001..."}

event: action
data: {"tool_name": "get_erp_data", "tool_input": {"action": "get_shipment", "shipment_id": "shp-001", ...}}

event: observation
data: {"content": "{\"id\": \"shp-001\", \"status\": \"in_transit\", \"origin\": \"Madrid, ES\", \"destination\": \"Paris, FR\", ...}"}

event: final_answer
data: {"content": "Shipment shp-001 (TRK-2024-000001) is currently in transit from Madrid, ES to Paris, FR via DHL Express."}

event: done
data: {"conversation_id": "<uuid>", "total_steps": 3}
```

### Seed data reference
- shp-001: `in_transit`, Madrid → Paris, DHL Express, user-001

## Tool chaining — UNDER_DECLARED case

Demonstrates the agent detecting a tax discrepancy.

### Tax reference
- Austria (AT) VAT = 20% → 5000 × 0.20 = **1000 EUR** expected
- Declared = 500 EUR → **UNDER_DECLARED by 500 EUR (-50%)**
- `alert: true` should be present in the tool result

### Seed data reference
- shp-006: `in_transit`, Vienna, AT → Prague, CZ, DHL Freight, user-003

## list_shipments action

Demonstrates `get_erp_data` with `action=list_shipments` and `status=in_transit`.

### Expected tool call
```json
{"action": "list_shipments", "status": "in_transit", "user_id": null, "shipment_id": null, "limit": null}
```

### Expected results
Shipments in_transit: shp-001 (Madrid→Paris), shp-006 (Vienna→Prague), shp-010 (Copenhagen→Oslo)

## Multi-turn conversation

Continues a previous conversation using `conversation_id` from the `done` SSE event.

**How to use:** Copy the `conversation_id` from the `done` event of any previous `/chat` request and set the `{{conversation_id}}` collection variable.

item.name: 2 · RAG Pipeline (Parte 2.2)
item.description: Tests for the RAG pipeline with metadata filtering (Parte 2.2 of the technical evaluation).

item.name: RAG — Search with year=2024 filter (metadata filtering)

request.description:
## RAG with Metadata Filtering — Parte 2.2 (CORE REQUIREMENT)

The query explicitly mentions 2024, which should trigger `search_regulations` with `metadata_filter: {"year": 2024}`.

### Documents with year=2024 in corpus
- `reg-003`: IATA DGR 65th Edition (dangerous_goods, GLOBAL, 2024)
- `reg-009`: EU Common External Tariff CN 2024 (customs, EU, 2024)
- `reg-012`: Last-Mile Delivery Urban Restrictions (urban_logistics, EU, 2024)

### Expected tool call
```json
{"query": "dangerous goods air transport 2024", "top_k": 3}
```
The metadata filter for year=2024 avoids returning older regulations (e.g. reg-001 from 2013, reg-007 from 1994) — demonstrating noise reduction in RAG retrieval.

item.name: RAG — Same query WITHOUT year filter (contrast test)

request.description:
## RAG WITHOUT year filter — contrast test

Same topic as the previous request but **without specifying a year**. Compare the observations to see that without metadata filtering the agent retrieves documents from multiple years (2013, 1994, 2024).

### Expected difference vs. the 2024-filtered request
- Without filter: may return reg-001 (2013), reg-007 (1994), reg-009 (2024)
- With year=2024 filter: only reg-009 (2024) for customs topic

This contrast **demonstrates the value of Metadata Filtering** to avoid noise.

## RAG with year=2021 filter

Demonstrates metadata filtering with a specific year other than 2024.

### Expected document
- `reg-011`: EU Dual-Use Regulation 2021/821 (export_controls, EU, 2021)

item.name: 3 · Error Handling (Parte 2.3)
item.description: Tests for the error handling requirements: LLM unavailable and ERP unreachable (Parte 2.3).

request.description:
## ERP not found — Parte 2.3

Requests shipment `shp-999` which does not exist in the seed data. The ERP adapter returns `null` and the agent must handle it gracefully.

### Expected behaviour
The agent receives a `not found` observation and communicates it to the user without crashing. The stream should complete with a `final_answer` event (not an `error` event), because the ERP responded — it just has no data for that ID.

item.name: Error — LLM unavailable (simulate with bad API key)

header.description: Not a real header — for documentation purposes. To test this: temporarily set an invalid OPENAI_API_KEY in .env and restart the server.

request.description:
## LLM Unavailable — Parte 2.3 (CORE REQUIREMENT)

**How to trigger:** Set `OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-invalid` in your `.env` and restart the server. Then send this request normally.

### Expected SSE error event
```
event: error
data: {
  "code": "LLM_UNAVAILABLE",
  "detail": "The AI model is temporarily unavailable. Please try again in a few moments.",
  "retry": true
}
```

### HTTP status: 200 (stream starts) — error is inside the SSE payload
The error is propagated as a structured SSE event, not as an HTTP error code, because the `StreamingResponse` has already begun.

item.name: Error — ERP unreachable (simulate with DB down)

request.description:
## ERP Unreachable — Parte 2.3 (CORE REQUIREMENT)

**How to trigger:** Stop the MySQL container (`docker stop react_db`) and send this request.

### Expected SSE error event
```
event: error
data: {
  "code": "ERP_UNAVAILABLE",
  "detail": "Could not retrieve data from the ERP system. The database may be unreachable.",
  "retry": false
}
```

### HTTP status: 200 (stream starts) — error is inside the SSE payload

item.name: 4 · Security — Prompt Injection & RBAC (Parte 3.1)
item.description: Tests for prompt injection detection, sensitive data blocking, and role-based access control (Parte 3.1).

request.description:
## Missing API key

No `X-API-Key` header sent.

### Expected response: `401 Unauthorized`
```json
{"code": "INVALID_API_KEY", "detail": "Missing or invalid X-API-Key header."}
```

## Invalid API key

Provides a well-formed but incorrect API key.

### Expected response: `401 Unauthorized`
```json
{"code": "INVALID_API_KEY", "detail": "Missing or invalid X-API-Key header."}
```

## Prompt injection — Parte 3.1 (CORE REQUIREMENT)

Pattern matched: `ignore.*previous.*instructions`

### Expected response: `400 Bad Request`
```json
{"code": "PROMPT_INJECTION", "detail": "Request blocked: potential prompt injection detected."}
```

## Prompt injection — DAN jailbreak attempt

Pattern matched: `\bDAN\b` and `you are now ... act as if`

### Expected response: `400 Bad Request`
```json
{"code": "PROMPT_INJECTION", "detail": "Request blocked: potential prompt injection detected."}
```

item.name: Security — Prompt injection: reveal system prompt (expect 400)

request.description:
## Prompt injection — system prompt extraction attempt

Pattern matched: `print.*system prompt.*instructions`

### Expected response: `400 Bad Request`
```json
{"code": "PROMPT_INJECTION", "detail": "Request blocked: potential prompt injection detected."}
```

## Sensitive data blocked — Parte 3.1 (CORE REQUIREMENT)

Keyword matched: `salary`

Note: even if this query somehow reached the ERP adapter, `salary` is in `_SENSITIVE_COLUMNS` and would be stripped from the SQL result.

### Expected response: `403 Forbidden`
```json
{"code": "SENSITIVE_DATA_BLOCKED", "detail": "Request blocked: query contains references to restricted data."}
```

## Sensitive data — password extraction attempt

Keyword matched: `password`

### Expected response: `403 Forbidden`
```json
{"code": "SENSITIVE_DATA_BLOCKED", "detail": "Request blocked: query contains references to restricted data."}
```

item.name: Security — RBAC: operator accessing financial report (expect 403)

request.description:
## RBAC violation — operator / financial report

Role `operator` is forbidden from accessing `financial_report`.

### Expected response: `403 Forbidden`
```json
{"code": "RBAC_VIOLATION", "detail": "Role 'operator' is not authorised to perform this action."}
```

## RBAC violation — viewer / mutation

Role `viewer` cannot perform delete/cancel/approve/reject operations.

### Expected response: `403 Forbidden`
```json
{"code": "RBAC_VIOLATION", "detail": "Role 'viewer' is not authorised to perform this action."}
```

item.name: Security — RBAC: auditor accessing personal data (expect 403)

request.description:
## RBAC violation — auditor / personal data

Role `auditor` cannot request `personal_data` or `get_user_profile` directly.

### Expected response: `403 Forbidden`
```json
{"code": "RBAC_VIOLATION", "detail": "Role 'auditor' is not authorised to perform this action."}
```

item.name: Security — RBAC: operator requesting list all shipments (expect 403)

request.description:
## RBAC violation — operator / bulk shipment list

Role `operator` is restricted to their own shipments and cannot query `list all shipments`.

### Expected response: `403 Forbidden`
```json
{"code": "RBAC_VIOLATION", "detail": "Role 'operator' is not authorised to perform this action."}
```

## Rate limiting

The sliding-window rate limiter allows max 20 requests per 60 seconds per API key.

**How to trigger:** Use Postman Runner to send this request 21+ times in quick succession.

### Expected response after limit exceeded: `429 Too Many Requests`
```json
{"detail": "Rate limit exceeded. Try again in N seconds."}
```

item.name: 5 · Conversation History
item.description: Tests for the conversation history endpoints.

request.description:
Retrieves the full message history of a conversation by its UUID.

**How to use:** Copy the `conversation_id` from the `done` SSE event of any `/chat` request and set the `{{conversation_id}}` collection variable.
Returns all conversations for `user-001` (alice.johnson, logistics_manager).

Requests a conversation UUID that does not exist.

### Expected response: `404 Not Found`
```json
{"detail": "Conversation not found"}
```
